entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A paramount crop for food and nutrition security in West Africa.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The sub-region where rice production is being improved.

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITION SECURITY
entities.description: The goal achieved through rice production in West African countries.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological factors that hamper rice production.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-biological factors that hamper rice production.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An abiotic stress factor that severely reduces grain yields of rice.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which is reduced by drought.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice varieties to maintain productivity under drought stress.

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of grain yield across different environments and conditions.

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci used for profiling and identification to improve drought resistance.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding process using markers to select for desired traits like drought tolerance.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding process using genome-wide markers to predict trait values.

entities.name: MULTI-LOCATIONAL YIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Extensive trials conducted across multiple locations to evaluate yield stability.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing improved rice varieties.
